test_description: On each masked, monopole/dipole-subtracted map compute pseudo-a_lm in the native Galactic basis for 2<=l<=20. For every adjacent pair with 2<=l<=19 and 1<=m<=l, form dphi_lm = arg(a_{l+1,m} a*_{l,m}) and its unit phasor exp(i dphi_lm). The scalar statistic is R = |sum exp(i dphi_lm)| / N, where N is the number of retained pairs, normally 189. Compare the Planck R to the same masked-pseudo-a_lm R distribution from the 1000 LCDM simulations; unusually large R indicates coherent low-l phase locking across neighbouring multipoles.
test_hypothesis: The Planck masked low-l pseudo-a_lm phases show anomalously strong adjacent-multipole phase coherence, i.e. Planck has a larger R than expected from LCDM simulations.
justificaton: The statistic R measures the concentration of adjacent phase-difference phasors, and the description/code explicitly state that unusually large R indicates coherent low-l phase locking. The reported tail of interest is the upper tail, so the directional anomaly is excess coherence rather than any deviation in either direction.

custom_summary.test_description: Masked Low-l Adjacent-Phase Coherence: after fitting and subtracting the monopole and dipole on the unmasked sky, set masked pixels to zero, compute masked pseudo-a_lm in Galactic/RING pixel basis for 2<=l<=20, and return R=|sum exp(i arg(a_{l+1,m} a^*_{l,m}))|/N over adjacent pairs 2<=l<=19 and 1<=m<=l.
custom_summary.statistic: R = |sum exp(i arg(a_{l+1,m} a^*_{l,m}))| / N for masked pseudo-a_lm adjacent low-l phases.
custom_summary.m_range: 1 <= m <= l; m=0 phases are deliberately excluded
custom_summary.tail_of_interest: upper tail: unusually large R indicates coherent phase locking across neighbouring low-l multipoles
custom_summary.masked_pixel_handling: finite pixels are used for the monopole/dipole fit; np.nan mask pixels are then filled with 0.0 before healpy.map2alm

plot_spec.title: Masked low-l adjacent-phase coherence
plot_spec.caption: Distribution of R from masked pseudo-a_lm for 2<=l<=20. Larger R means the phase differences arg(a_{l+1,m} a^*_{l,m}) are more aligned across adjacent multipoles.

custom_summary.interpretation: Planck is in the highest 5% of simulations: adjacent low-l pseudo-a_lm phase differences are unusually coherent.

test_summary.Description: Computes masked, monopole/dipole-subtracted pseudo-a_lm phases for 2<=l<=20 and measures R, the mean resultant length of adjacent-multipole phase-difference phasors arg(a_{l+1,m}a*_{l,m}) over m>=1. Larger R indicates stronger low-l phase locking.
test_summary.Results: Planck has R=0.1388 versus simulation mean 0.0665 and median 0.0636. It ranks at the 97th percentile, with 30/1000 simulations larger; one-tailed upper p=0.03, corresponding to about 1.88 sigma.
test_summary.Interpretation: Planck shows unusually high adjacent low-l phase coherence relative to the LCDM masked-pseudo-a_lm simulations, significant at the 5% one-tailed level but not at 1% and only marginally below 2 sigma.
test_summary.Comparison to literature: I am not aware of a directly comparable published test using this exact masked low-l adjacent-multipole pseudo-a_lm phase-coherence statistic, and prior searches found no close match; this should not be treated as a repeat of a standard anomaly statistic.
